name: LLM Research and Documentation
description: Research the top {{param.count}} LLMs based on {{param.criteria}}, gather pricing information, and create individual Notion pages for each model.

paramConfigs.name: count
paramConfigs.description: Number of top LLMs to research

agents.name: AI Research Specialist
agents.role: Expert in AI/ML models, benchmarks, and LLM capabilities with focus on performance metrics and pricing

agents.name: Documentation Specialist
agents.role: Expert in creating structured, detailed documentation with clear formatting

stepConfigs.name: research-llms
stepConfigs.instructions:
# Research Top LLMs

Your task is to identify the top {{param.count}} large language models (LLMs) based on {{param.criteria}}.

## Requirements

1. Search for the latest LLM benchmarks and evaluations focusing on {{param.criteria}}
2. Consider models from major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, etc.)
3. For each of the top {{param.count}} models, gather model details.
4. **CRITICAL**: Your ENTIRE response must be valid JSON and nothing else.

Return a JSON array:
[
  {"model_name": "Model Name", "provider": "Provider", "capabilities": "...", "performance": "...", "pricing": "...", "api_access": "...", "notes": "..."}
]

stepConfigs.name: setup-notion
stepConfigs.instructions:
# Setup Notion Workspace

You will receive a list of LLM models: {{step.research-llms.output}}

1. Browse the Notion workspace to understand the existing structure
2. Find an appropriate parent page OR create a new one
3. Add the parent_page_id field to each model object
4. **CRITICAL**: Your ENTIRE response must be valid JSON and nothing else.

stepConfigs.name: create-page
stepConfigs.instructions: Create a Notion page for {{item.model_name}} by {{item.provider}} under parent {{item.parent_page_id}} with full content.
